clink_morphisms: Elementary particles bind through fundamental forces to form hadrons.
Hadrons coalesce via residual strong interaction to form atomic nuclei.
Atomic nuclei attract electrons through electromagnetic interaction to form atoms.
Atoms bond chemically through electron sharing or transfer to form molecules.
Molecules polymerize or assemble noncovalently to form macromolecules.
Macromolecules organize structurally and functionally to form organelles.
Organelles integrate within a semipermeable membrane to form a cell.
Cells coordinate through biochemical signaling to form a multicellular organism.
Organisms interact trophically and spatially to form an ecosystem.
Ecosystems couple with geophysical systems to form a planet.
Planets gravitationally bind with stellar and interstellar components to form the cosmos.
